dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6945 : Supporting Pregnant and Parenting Women and Families Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Received; read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend part A of title IV of the Social Security Act to clarify the authority of States to use funds for pregnancy centers, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Supporting Pregnant and Parenting Women and Families Act.

section.header: Prohibition on discrimination against pregnancy centers
section.text: Section 404 of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 604) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subsection.header: Use of funds for pregnancy centers

paragraph.text: Nothing in this part shall be construed to prohibit a State from using a grant made under section 403 to support pregnancy centers.
In paragraph (1), the term pregnancy center means any organization, such as a pregnancy resource center, pregnancy help center or organization, or pregnancy medical center, that—

subparagraph.text: supports protecting the life of the mother and the unborn child; and

offers resources and services to mothers, fathers, and families, including but not limited to relationship counseling, prenatal and pregnancy education, pregnancy testing, diapers, baby clothes, or other material supports.

attestation-group.attestation-date: Passed the House of Representatives January 21, 2026.
